report_name: Cyber Sovereignty and Supply Chain Risk Become Top Concerns for Critical Infrastructure

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Adversaries exploit third-party vendor relationships to launch attacks against critical infrastructure, leveraging hidden dependencies in the software and hardware supply chain.

tools.name: Software Bill of Materials
tools.description: Used as a key mechanism for transparency to identify all components, including open-source libraries and dependencies, within a piece of software to manage supply chain risk.

tools.name: Software Composition Analysis tools
tools.description: Used to analyze Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs) for known vulnerabilities and risky dependencies in the software supply chain.

identities.name: Critical National Infrastructure
identities.description: Operators of critical national infrastructure (CNI) are identified as being at the center of a strategic shift towards prioritizing supply chain security and cyber sovereignty. Specific sectors mentioned include Energy, Water and Wastewater Systems, Transportation, Telecommunications, Healthcare, and Finance.
